title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Allergies
intro: Herbal teas for allergies are natural infusions made from various herbs and plants that help alleviate symptoms associated with allergies, such as congestion, sneezing, and itchy eyes. These teas can be effective in treating allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the body's allergic response and promote overall well-being. Examples of herbal teas used to treat allergies include Nettle Leaf tea, which is rich in antioxidants and helps to reduce inflammation; Quercetin tea, which is a natural antihistamine that blocks the release of histamine; Echinacea tea, which is known for its immune-boosting properties and helps to fight off infections; Peppermint tea, which helps to ease congestion and soothe the nasal passages; Ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain; and Licorice root tea, which is a natural expectorant that helps to thin mucus and promote drainage. By incorporating these herbal teas into your daily routine, you can find natural relief from allergy symptoms and promote a healthier, more balanced lifestyle.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat various allergy-related ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The bioactive constituents present in Eucalyptus globulus teas, such as eucalyptol, limonene, and pinene, help to treat allergies by reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and acting as a natural expectorant. The herbal preparation helps to treat allergies by promoting the drainage of mucus from the nasal passages and airways, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as sneezing, coughing, and runny nose. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat allergies include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a safer alternative to conventional medications and a valuable addition to a holistic treatment plan.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Eucalyptus leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Then, carefully remove the tea infuser or muslin bag.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it while it is still warm. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Eucalyptus globulus teas for allergies may be beneficial due to its ability to inhibit mast-cell degranulation, with its alcoholic extracts showing an inhibitory effect of more than 80% in histamine release from RBL-2H3 cells.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for alleviating allergy symptoms, but it may cause some side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals who are sensitive to the compound eucalyptol. To use Eucalyptus globulus teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and be cautious when taking it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Adjust dosage to 1.5 teaspoons per cup if symptoms persist.
Consume Eucalyptus globulus tea for 7-10 days for optimal results.
Combine with other allergy-fighting herbs for enhanced relief.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat allergies, utilizing their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to provide relief. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas help to modulate the immune response, reducing the production of histamine and other inflammatory mediators that contribute to allergic reactions. By inhibiting the release of inflammatory chemicals and scavenging free radicals, Ginkgo biloba teas alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with allergies. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat allergies include reduced severity of symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural, non-pharmacological approach to managing this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Measure 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using the infuser or a strainer. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably in the morning and evening, to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can cause potential side effects when used to treat allergies, including stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas include being cautious of interactions with medications like blood thinners and antacids, avoiding use in individuals with bleeding disorders or high blood pressure, and not consuming excessive amounts which can lead to headaches and dizziness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250-500mg of Ginkgo biloba per cup.
Increase dose to 1-2 tablespoons if needed, after 2 weeks.
Take tea 30 minutes before meals for best absorption.
Consult doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after 4 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including allergies. The anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate allergy symptoms, such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. Bioactive constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea teas exhibit potent antioxidant and antihistamine activities, which contribute to their therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas has been shown to enhance the body's natural defenses, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from allergy-related discomforts.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add honey to taste if needed. Stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas for allergies may have some benefits compared to a placebo, but there is not enough evidence to recommend their use for treating or preventing common colds, which can be associated with allergies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, especially when consumed in high doses or for extended periods. Precautions to take when using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat allergies include being cautious with its use in people with autoimmune disorders, such as rheumatoid arthritis or lupus, and monitoring for allergic reactions, especially if you are already taking medications for allergies.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor or healthcare professional for guidance on using Echinacea purpurea tea.
Start with 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day, as recommended.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage to 2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day if needed.
Continue using Echinacea purpurea tea for 7-14 days to see noticeable allergy relief.
Discontinue use after 14 days and consult doctor for further guidance if symptoms persist.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderflower tea, are a natural remedy used to treat allergies, particularly hay fever and sinusitis. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat allergies include its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which help to reduce congestion and alleviate symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, with flavonoids being particularly effective in reducing inflammation and stabilizing mast cells. Drinking Sambucus nigra tea can provide benefits in treating allergies by reducing symptoms, preventing inflammation, and promoting overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Sambucus nigra flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried Sambucus nigra flowers in a tea strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled water over the Sambucus nigra flowers in the tea strainer.
Steep the Sambucus nigra flowers for 5-10 minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups throughout the day, as needed for allergy relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be an effective treatment for allergies, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, it's essential to be aware of potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and to avoid consuming it with other herbal preparations that may have synergistic effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of Sambucus nigra dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Start with low dosage (1 cup) and adjust as needed.
Continue using Sambucus nigra tea for 2-4 weeks for best results.
Consult a healthcare professional for specific allergy treatment guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat allergies due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, thereby alleviating symptoms such as congestion, sneezing, and itchy eyes. The bioactive constituents present in Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm the body's allergic response. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat allergies include reduced symptoms, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in stress and anxiety levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and place in a tea infuser.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water and pour over the dried leaves in the infuser.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink as needed to help with allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help alleviate allergy symptoms, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, digestive issues, and skin irritation in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation to treat allergies include being cautious of interactions with medications, avoiding use in children and pregnant women, and monitoring for signs of allergic reactions such as hives or difficulty breathing.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea daily in the morning.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia leaves per cup.
Use tea for 7-10 days to relieve allergy symptoms.
Repeat for 3-4 weeks to experience long-term relief.
Consult a doctor before changing your allergy treatment plan.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to alleviate allergy symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, thereby providing relief from allergic reactions. By inhibiting the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators, ginger tea helps to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with allergies. Regular consumption of ginger tea has been shown to provide benefits in managing allergy symptoms, including reduced severity of allergic reactions and improved respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle.
Measure 1 teaspoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (Ginger) root and chop it finely.
Add the chopped ginger to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiled water over the ginger in the tea infuser or cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup and drink.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas may help alleviate symptoms of allergies, including rhinorrhea, sneezing, and itching, by acting on mechanisms and mediators that cause allergic reactions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to alleviate allergy symptoms, but its prolonged consumption may cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its potential to irritate the stomach lining. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and refrain from using it in conjunction with other medications or supplements that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2 times a day for allergy relief.
Consume 1/2 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder 3 times a day with meals.
Add 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale root to a warm bath for 20 minutes.
Use 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale oil in a diffuser for 30 minutes.
Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and frequency.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and volatile oils, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies. By consuming Achillea millefolium tea, individuals can experience relief from allergy-related symptoms such as congestion, itching, and skin rashes, as it helps to modulate the immune system and decrease the production of histamine. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium tea to treat allergies include its natural and non-addictive properties, making it a safe and effective alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves.
Use 2 tablespoons of the leaves for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat allergies, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, especially when consumed in large amounts or for extended periods. To use Achillea millefolium teas safely for allergy treatment, it is essential to brew the tea according to the recommended instructions, avoid taking it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds, and start with a low dose to monitor your body's response.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Achillea millefolium teas for allergies.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Achillea millefolium per 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week.
Take 1-2 cups of tea at least 2 hours before meals.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust dosage as recommended by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas, also known as German chamomile, have been traditionally used to treat various allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal preparation's ability to calm the skin and reduce histamine release helps to alleviate symptoms of allergic reactions such as itching, redness, and swelling. The bioactive constituents present in Matricaria chamomilla teas, including apigenin and luteolin, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Matricaria chamomilla teas may help to reduce the severity and frequency of allergic reactions, providing relief and comfort to individuals suffering from this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers. If using fresh, make sure they are clean and dry.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a pot. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Pour the cooled water over the tea infuser or muslin bag containing the Matricaria chamomilla flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if needed. Drink 2-3 cups a day for relief from allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be a potential remedy for allergy symptoms, but using it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, especially those with ragweed pollen allergy. To use Matricaria chamomilla teas safely, it is essential to consume it in moderation and be aware of potential interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, and to avoid driving or operating heavy machinery after consumption.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Matricaria chamomilla tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain and serve.
Take 1 cup, 30 minutes before meals for optimal relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme tea, have been used for centuries to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum, including thymol, carvacrol, and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, thereby alleviating allergy symptoms such as congestion and itching. The tea's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from respiratory allergies. The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat allergies include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Thymus serpyllum leaves.
Combine 1 cup of leaves with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids. Let it cool.
Drink 1 cup of the tea up to 3 times a day to help with allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can cause stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. When using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat allergies, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and monitoring your body's response to prevent potential interactions with other medications or exacerbating underlying health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Thymus serpyllum tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a small dose of 1 tablespoon of dried herbs per cup.
Gradually increase dose to 2 tablespoons per cup as needed.
Use Thymus serpyllum tea for 7-10 days for optimal allergy relief.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Thymus serpyllum tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas have been traditionally used to treat allergies, particularly those related to respiratory issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, thereby alleviating symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit histamine release and modulate the immune response, contributing to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Plantago lanceolata teas to treat allergies include its natural and non-pharmacological approach, making it a popular choice among those seeking alternative remedies for this common condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves. You can find them in most health food stores or grow them yourself.
Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Pat them dry with a clean towel.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the dried leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Allergy, Plantago lanceolata teas for allergies may be beneficial in treating symptoms related to allergies, particularly those associated with melon and other fruits or vegetables, as the study found a significant prevalence of sensitivity to Plantago in patients allergic to melon and other food sources.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Plantago lanceolata teas can be used to treat allergies, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach cramps, as well as allergic reactions such as skin rashes and itching. When using Plantago lanceolata teas to treat allergies, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, monitoring for any adverse reactions, and avoiding use if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies, especially to plants in the Plantago genus.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing histamine release and modulating the immune system's response to allergens. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. Regular consumption of dandelion tea may help to reduce symptoms of allergies, such as congestion, itching, and skin rashes, while also promoting overall immune system health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 1 tablespoon of the gathered Taraxacum officinale to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink while warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Taraxacum officinale teas for allergies are not mentioned in the provided study, however, the study actually mentions that an active immunomodulator was isolated from the methanol extract of Leucas lavandulifolia and identified as taraxerone, which exhibits immunosuppressive activity and is capable of modulating the immune system, proving to be a potent drug for the treatment of many allergic disorders.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat allergic reactions, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as allergic reactions to the herbal preparation itself. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large amounts, as this may exacerbate allergic reactions, and to store the herbal preparation in a cool, dark place to preserve its potency.

preparations.herb_desc: Nasturtium officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat allergies, and their properties make them an effective herbal preparation for this ailment. The teas exhibit anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the severity of allergic reactions by suppressing the production of histamine and other inflammatory mediators. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and isothiocyanates, play a crucial role in treating allergies by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress. By consuming Nasturtium officinale teas, individuals can experience relief from allergy symptoms, such as itching, sneezing, and congestion, and also enjoy the benefits of improved respiratory health and enhanced immune function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Nasturtium officinale flowers and leaves. Wash them in cold water.
Dry the flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 10-15 minutes.
Make a tea by steeping 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea with a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Nasturtium officinale teas can be used as a potential treatment for allergies, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, as well as allergic reactions, like hives and itching. Precautions should be taken when using Nasturtium officinale teas to treat allergies, such as starting with small doses, avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and monitoring for any signs of an allergic reaction or digestive issues, as the effects may vary from person to person.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat allergies by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as sneezing, congestion, and itchy eyes. The properties of this herbal preparation help to modulate the immune system, thereby reducing the severity of allergic reactions. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Drinking Echinacea angustifolia teas regularly may help to build up the body's resistance to allergies and reduce the reliance on conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of water.
Combine the root and water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture on low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup.
Add honey to taste. You can also add lemon juice if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Echinacea angustifolia teas for allergies may exacerbate allergies and asthma due to the risk of anaphylactic reactions and hepatotoxicity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat allergies, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. When using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat allergies, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid taking it for extended periods of time to minimize the risk of interactions with other medications and potential allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies, including itching, redness, and congestion. By consuming nettle tea, individuals can experience relief from allergy symptoms as it helps to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic reactions, and promotes the body's natural anti-inflammatory response. The benefits of using Urtica dioica teas to treat allergies include reduced reliance on over-the-counter medications and a natural approach to managing symptoms, making it a popular choice among those seeking alternative remedies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the Urtica dioica leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Pour the boiling water over the Urtica dioica leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 to 1 cup of it 2-3 times a day to help with allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of pharmacy & bioallied sciences, Urtica dioica teas for allergies have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic effects, as well as decreased paw thickness, which validates anti-allergic properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be beneficial in treating allergies, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and skin irritation in some individuals due to its high histamine content. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, take it in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be cautious with its use if you have sensitive skin or digestive issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat various allergies, including hay fever and asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, glycosides, and triterpenoids, found in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies. By inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat allergies include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications and a natural, non-invasive approach to managing symptoms and promoting overall health and well-being.

Crush the roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the crushed roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for allergies have displayed adverse effects, usually in the form of a hypersensitivity reaction.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat allergies, but it may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and an increased risk of heart problems due to its licorice root content. When using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat allergies, take precautions such as limiting the dose and duration of consumption to avoid prolonged exposure to its active compounds, and also be cautious when combining it with other medications that may interact with its effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat allergies, leveraging their anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties to alleviate symptoms. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from allergic reactions. By inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators, Artemisia absinthium teas can help to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with allergies. The benefits of using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat allergies include their non-pharmacological nature, ease of preparation, and potential for long-term use with minimal side effects.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Discard the leaves and drink the tea, or use a tea infuser.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with allergies.
preparations.preparation_study: According to AMB Express, Artemisia absinthium teas for allergies may not be a suitable option as the study focuses on the antimicrobial activities of Artemisia species, particularly Artemisia absinthium against bacterial strains, and does not mention their effect on allergies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat allergies, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of thujone, a compound that can be toxic in large amounts. Precautions to take when using these teas include limiting the dosage to 1-2 cups per day, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and being cautious if you have a history of liver or kidney problems, as this may exacerbate existing conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, also known as sage tea, have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including allergies. The antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis help to alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing the release of histamine and soothing the skin and mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, exhibit antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. By consuming Salvia officinalis teas, individuals can experience relief from allergy symptoms, including itching, sneezing, and congestion, and also benefit from its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and prevent infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for allergies do not appear to be mentioned in the provided study, which focuses on the antibacterial properties and potential as adjuvants for antibiotic treatment of the selected herbs.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat allergies, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, nausea, and headaches due to the high sage content that can interact with other medications. Precautions should be taken when consuming Salvia officinalis teas, including drinking plenty of water to prevent dehydration, avoiding overconsumption, and monitoring for any adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Verbascum thapsus teas, also known as mullein teas, have been traditionally used to treat allergies and related respiratory issues. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, help to reduce symptoms such as congestion, sneezing, and itchy eyes. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, in Verbascum thapsus teas help to inhibit the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators, providing relief from allergic reactions. Regular consumption of Verbascum thapsus teas may also help to strengthen the immune system and promote overall well-being, making it a beneficial natural remedy for managing allergies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Verbascum thapsus flowers, leaves, and stems. Clean them with water.
Dry the Verbascum thapsus in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1 hour. Let them cool.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Verbascum thapsus in a teapot. Add 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the Verbascum thapsus tea 2-3 times a day to help with allergies. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 2 days.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Verbascum thapsus teas can be used as a potential remedy for allergies, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and dizziness in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat allergies, including avoiding it during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as monitoring for interactions with other medications, and starting with low doses to assess tolerance.

preparations.herb_desc: Rumex crispus teas, also known as dock leaf tea, have been traditionally used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents of Rumex crispus, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, help to alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response. By consuming Rumex crispus teas, individuals may experience relief from symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with allergies. The benefits of using Rumex crispus teas to treat allergies include reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced overall immune system function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rumex crispus leaves and stems. Rinse them with clean water.
Dry the leaves and stems in a warm, dark place for 1-2 days or use a food dehydrator.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Rumex crispus leaves and stems into a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the leaves and stems in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rumex crispus teas can be used to treat allergies, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and nausea due to its high content of oxalic acid and other compounds that may interact with the body's response to allergens. When using Rumex crispus teas for allergy treatment, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's reaction closely, and avoid consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods to minimize potential side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been traditionally used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The berberine content in goldenseal helps to treat allergies by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents, including berberine, hydrastine, and canadine, work together to inhibit the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators, providing relief from symptoms such as congestion and itching. The benefits of goldenseal teas in treating allergies include reduced symptoms and improved quality of life, making it a popular herbal remedy for seasonal and environmental allergies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tsp of dried Hydrastis canadensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the root.
Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Allergy and asthma proceedings, Hydrastis canadensis teas for allergies have been found to have anti-allergy components with effects on bronchodilation, congestion, and antagonism of asthma mediators such as histamine, which may help alleviate allergy symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to alleviate allergy symptoms, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps due to its high content of berberine. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume these teas in moderation, under 1 cup per day, and to monitor your body's response to adjust the dosage accordingly, as excessive consumption may exacerbate symptoms.

preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Eucalyptus globulus teas for allergies show potential in inhibiting histamine release from mast cells, demonstrating an inhibitory effect of more than 80% at a concentration of 0.5 mg/ml.
intro_study: According to "International archives of allergy and immunology", teas for allergies using the leaves and stems of plant Anchietia salutaris can be effective in treating allergies due to its ability to inhibit histamine release in rat and guinea pig tissues.
